content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFO RNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
'/ (916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
7 
JuliaM. Lew 
City Attorney 
City of Porterville 
McConnick, Kabot, Jenner & Lew 
4010 South Demaree Street 
Visalia, CA 93277 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A17-243 
Dear Ms. Lew: 
December 6, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Porterville City Council Member 
Monte Reyes regarding Government Code Section 1090, et seq. 1 Please note that we do not advise 
on any other area oflaw, including Public Contract Code or common law conflicts of interest. We 
are also not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71), and any 
advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Tulare County 
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.l(c)(3).) We did not receive a 
written response from either entity. (Section I 097 .1 ( c )( 4 ). ) We are also required to advise you that, 
for purposes of Section I 090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1 ( c )( 5).) 
QUESTION 
Would Section 1090 prohibit future funding contracts between the City of Porterville and 
the Chamber of Commerce, if Councilmember Reyes were hired as the Chief Executive Officer 
("CEO") for the Chamber? 
CONCLUSION 
No. The City may continue to enter into funding contracts with the Chamber in the event 
that Council Member Reyes is hired as the CEO of the Chamber. However, as Council Member 
Reyes would have a remote interest in the contact as an officer or employee of a nonprofit 
1 Government Code Sections 1090 through 1097.5. All statutory references are to the Government Code, 
unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 
through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of 
the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-17-243 
Page No. 2 
corporation, he would be required to follow the disclosure and abstention requirements of Section 
I 091 on any contract decisions relating to the Chamber that come before the City Council. 
FACTS 
Porterville City Councilmember Reyes is considering a salaried position as the Chief 
Executive Officer for the Chamber of Commerce of Porterville ("Chamber"). According to the 
California Secretary of State, the Chamber is an active domestic nonprofit corporation, first 
registered in 1912 with amendments to its Articles in 2011.2 
The City has an ongoing year-to-year funding agreement ("Agreement") with the Chamber 
to carry on certain programs and promotions activities, hold certain activities at a specific City Park, 
and provide an annual activity report. As consideration for these activities, the City provides 
funding of$35,000.00 per year to the Chamber. The Agreement began in 2006 and continues on a 
year-to year basis unless terminated. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, 
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. The prohibition applies even when 
the terms of the proposed contract are demonstrably fair and equitable, or are plainly to the public 
entity's advantage. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646-649.) Section 1090 is intended "not 
only to strike at actual impropriety, but also to strike at the appearance of impropriety." (City of 
Imperial Beach v. Bailey ( 1980) 103 Cal.App.3d 191, 197.) A contract that violates Section 1090 is 
void. (Thomson, supra, at p. 646.) 
As a general rule, when Section 1090 is applicable to one member of a governing body of a 
public entity, the prohibition cannot be avoided by having the interested board member abstain; the 
entire governing body is precluded from entering into the contract. (Thomson v. Call, supra, at pp. 
647-649; Stigall v. City of Taft, supra, at p. 569; 86 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 138, 139 (2003); 70 
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 45, 48 (1987).) However, the Legislature has created various statutory 
exceptions to Section 1090's prohibition, including where the financial interest involved is deemed 
to be a "remote interest," as defined in Section 1091. 
If a "remote interest" is present, the contract may be made if ( 1) the official in question 
discloses his or her financial interest in the contract to the public agency, (2) such interest is noted 
in the entity's official records, and (3) the official abstains from any participation in the making of 
the contract. (Section 109l(a); 88 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 106, 108 (2005); 83 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 246, 
248 (2000).) 
Section l 091 (b )( 1) defines a remote interest as: "[ t]hat of an officer or employee of a . .. 
nonprofit corporation, except as provided in 1091.5(a)(8)." The Chamber is organized as a 
2 You state that you are uncertain as to whether the Chamber is a 50l(c)(3) or 50l(c)(6) organization. 
However, the remote inlerest in Section 1091(b)(l) applies to "nonprofit corporations," and is not limited to 501(c)(3) 
organizations. 


File No. A-17-243 
Page No. 3 
nonprofit corporation. 3 Pursuant to Section l 091 (b )( l ), the Councilmember' s financial interest as 
the salaried Chamber CEO is a remote interest. Accordingly, City council decisions involving 
contracts with the Chamber, such as the Agreement, may be made as long as Councilmember Reyes 
discloses his financial interest, the interest is noted in the council's official records, and he does not 
participate in the making of the decision. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
ZWN:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
3. "YJk 
By: 
Zachary W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
3 Section I 09 l .5(a)(8) applies to noncompensated positions, and is not applicable here.

sections.question:
Would Section 1090 prohibit future funding contracts between the City of Porterville and 
the Chamber of Commerce, if Councilmember Reyes were hired as the Chief Executive Officer 
("CEO") for the Chamber?
sections.conclusion:
No. The City may continue to enter into funding contracts with the Chamber in the event 
that Council Member Reyes is hired as the CEO of the Chamber. However, as Council Member 
Reyes would have a remote interest in the contact as an officer or employee of a nonprofit 
1 Government Code Sections 1090 through 1097.5. corporation, he would be required to follow the disclosure and abstention requirements of Section 
I 091 on any contract decisions relating to the Chamber that come before the City Council.

nonprofit corporation. 3 Pursuant to Section l 091 (b )( l ), the Councilmember' s financial interest as

QUESTION: Would Section 1090 prohibit future funding contracts between the City of Porterville and 
the Chamber of Commerce, if Councilmember Reyes were hired as the Chief Executive Officer 
("CEO") for the Chamber?

CONCLUSION: No. The City may continue to enter into funding contracts with the Chamber in the event 
that Council Member Reyes is hired as the CEO of the Chamber. However, as Council Member 
Reyes would have a remote interest in the contact as an officer or employee of a nonprofit 
1 Government Code Sections 1090 through 1097.5. corporation, he would be required to follow the disclosure and abstention requirements of Section 
I 091 on any contract decisions relating to the Chamber that come before the City Council.
